explanation: Part 1 uses public stated-values web pages, while Part 2 uses proxy statements. These documents have different audiences and purposes, so perfect alignment is not expected.
The index is only calculated when both stated-values text and proxy text are available. Rows with missing or unusable text are retained but labeled as insufficient data.
Theme categories rely on predefined keyword dictionaries. This improves transparency, but it may miss subtle or indirect language about corporate values.
Tone alignment is based on positive and negative word counts. It should be read as a simple supporting signal rather than a complete sentiment analysis.
The authenticity index measures language alignment. It does not prove that a company actually behaves authentically or that specific events caused changes in the score.
